content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Added daemon-side cancellation handoff/finalization so a cancelled daemon-owned run is marked interrupted and has `run.pid` removed before the shutdown grace period expires, then finalized without duplicating terminal journal state. Tightened stale-owner recovery to fail closed when detached worktree or lease cleanup returns `AlreadyAbsent`, preserved durable task/lease state on partial cleanup, added regression coverage for both behaviors, and closed bead `ralph-burning-rlm.5` in `.beads/issues.jsonl`.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the daemon dispatch cancellation path, stale-owner recovery helpers, and bead context to identify where interrupted state was only persisted after the grace timeout and where partial cleanup outcomes were being ignored.
2. [Completed] Implemented immediate daemon cancellation handoff/finalization in `daemon_loop.rs`, tightened stale-owner cleanup handling in `cli_writer_lease.rs`, and added/updated regression tests in `daemon_loop.rs`, `cli_writer_lease.rs`, and `cli/run.rs`.
3. [Completed] Validated the changes with repository checks, updated bead state, and synced bead metadata.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy` passed (exit 0); it still emits existing repository warnings unrelated to this change.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `br lint` was run and reports only pre-existing unrelated template warnings on other beads.
- `br close --actor "${BR_ACTOR:-assistant}" ralph-burning-rlm.5 --reason "Implemented daemon SIGTERM handoff cleanup and fail-closed stale-owner cleanup"` succeeded.

## Outstanding Risks

- `br lint` still reports unrelated pre-existing template warnings on other beads (`ralph-burning-rlm.1`, `ralph-burning-rlm.2`, `ralph-burning-rlm.3`, `ralph-burning-utm`, `ralph-burning-kvc`, `ralph-burning-gsp`, `ralph-burning-p2h`, `ralph-burning-fyj`, `ralph-burning-hg4`).
